phrases.intent_en: Ask to taste the local Touraine Amboise wine
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais goûter le Touraine-Amboise, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DRAY goo-TAY luh too-REN ahm-BWAZ, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Touraine-Amboise is a local AOC wine appellation. The cave is carved into the limestone cliffs (tuffeau) beneath the town.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the difference between the red and the rosé
phrases.text_local: Quelle est la différence entre le rouge et le rosé ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel ay lah dee-fay-RAHNS AHN-truh luh ROOZH ay luh roh-ZAY
phrases.cultural_note: Touraine-Amboise reds are primarily Côt (Malbec) or Cabernet Franc; the rosés are lighter and often pétillant (slightly sparkling).

responses.english: The red is fuller-bodied, the rosé lighter.
responses.phonetic: luh ROOZH eh ploo kor-SAY, luh roh-ZAY ploo lay-ZHAY
responses.french: Le rouge est plus corsé, le rosé plus léger.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if they ship wine to the United States
phrases.cultural_note: Many Loire Valley caves sell wine to export, but international shipping regulations vary. They can advise on purchasing for travel.

responses.english: No, but you can carry it in your suitcase.
responses.phonetic: nohn, meh voo poo-VAY ahm-por-TAY ahn vah-LEEZ
responses.french: Non, mais vous pouvez emporter en valise.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what temperature the wine cellar is
phrases.text_local: Quelle température fait-il dans la cave ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel tahm-pay-rah-TOOR feh-TEEL dahn lah KAHV
phrases.cultural_note: The caves (caves troglodytiques) maintain a constant temperature of around 12°C, ideal for wine storage.

responses.english: About twelve degrees all year round.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a bottle recommendation to pair with cheese
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous me recommander une bouteille pour accompagner un plateau de fromages ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY-voo muh ruh-koh-mahn-DAY oon boo-TAY poor ah-kom-pahn-YAY un plah-TOH duh froh-MAZH
phrases.cultural_note: Touraine whites (Chenin Blanc) pair beautifully with local goat cheeses. Vendors are accustomed to giving pairing advice.

responses.phonetic: zhuh voo kohn-SAY luh shuh-NAN BLAHN
responses.french: Je vous conseille le Chenin Blanc.
